piece.title: Broadway Boogie Woogie (FormaComposition) v7

m1_grid_1.name: m1_grid_1
m1_grid_1.notes: I. Grid — 4 min at 110 BPM. Bass alone. Mute melody and harmony in Logic. | Harmony fix: V1 (ii-v-I) x4 — corrected from II-V-I; diatonic minor ii/v, no borrowed majors. | Decouple pass (v3): melody=motif (sole owner of the cell), bass=steady (locked figure from STEADY_FIGURES, chord- and meter-bound, approach notes into changes), harmony=sustain (held color-field chords, length = harmonic span), swing=0.34 at section level (public 0.0-1.0 scale; remap_swing_ratio(0.34)=0.67 internal = triplet swing — the v2 value 0.67 was on the internal scale and would have produced a 0.835 hard shuffle).

m1_grid_2.name: m1_grid_2
m1_grid_2.notes: I. Grid — 4 min at 110 BPM. Bass alone. Mute melody and harmony in Logic. | Harmony fix: V1 (ii-v-I) — corrected from II-V-I. | Decouple pass (v3): melody=motif (sole owner of the cell), bass=steady (locked figure from STEADY_FIGURES, chord- and meter-bound, approach notes into changes), harmony=sustain (held color-field chords, length = harmonic span), swing=0.34 at section level (public 0.0-1.0 scale; remap_swing_ratio(0.34)=0.67 internal = triplet swing — the v2 value 0.67 was on the internal scale and would have produced a 0.835 hard shuffle).

m1_grid_3.name: m1_grid_3
m1_grid_3.notes: I. Grid — 4 min at 110 BPM. Bass alone. Mute melody and harmony in Logic. | Harmony check: already diatonic (I-v7-ii-v7), no change needed. | Decouple pass (v3): melody=motif (sole owner of the cell), bass=steady (locked figure from STEADY_FIGURES, chord- and meter-bound, approach notes into changes), harmony=sustain (held color-field chords, length = harmonic span), swing=0.34 at section level (public 0.0-1.0 scale; remap_swing_ratio(0.34)=0.67 internal = triplet swing — the v2 value 0.67 was on the internal scale and would have produced a 0.835 hard shuffle).

m2_accumulation.name: m2_accumulation
m2_accumulation.notes: II. Accumulation — 5 min at 110 BPM. Bass + offbeat comping stabs. Mute melody in Logic. | Harmony fix: V3 (I-bVII-ii-v) x3 — bVII as Mondrian color block, diatonic, no functional pull. | Decouple pass (v3): melody=motif (sole owner of the cell), bass=steady (locked figure from STEADY_FIGURES, chord- and meter-bound, approach notes into changes), harmony=motif (stressed filter on the shaped v3 cell -> five varied-length on-beat comp chords per 2-bar cycle — its own syncopated life against the bass), swing=0.34 at section level (public 0.0-1.0 scale; remap_swing_ratio(0.34)=0.67 internal = triplet swing — the v2 value 0.67 was on the internal scale and would have produced a 0.835 hard shuffle).

m3_canon_entry.name: m3_canon_entry
m3_canon_entry.notes: III. Canon (entry) — melodic cell arrives. Duplicate counterpoint MIDI region, offset 2 beats right for the canon effect. | Harmony fix: V4 truncated (ii-iii°-v) — diminished passing chord leads into canon entry, deliberately unresolved. | Decouple pass (v3): melody=motif (sole owner of the cell), bass=steady (locked figure from STEADY_FIGURES, chord- and meter-bound, approach notes into changes), harmony=sustain (held color-field chords, length = harmonic span), swing=0.34 at section level (public 0.0-1.0 scale; remap_swing_ratio(0.34)=0.67 internal = triplet swing — the v2 value 0.67 was on the internal scale and would have produced a 0.835 hard shuffle).

m3_canon_full.name: m3_canon_full
m3_canon_full.notes: III. Canon (full) — both voices in flight. | Harmony fix: V4 (ii-iii°-v-I) x3 — diatonic passing motion under full canon. | Decouple pass (v3): melody=motif (sole owner of the cell), bass=steady (locked figure from STEADY_FIGURES, chord- and meter-bound, approach notes into changes), harmony=sustain (held color-field chords, length = harmonic span), swing=0.34 at section level (public 0.0-1.0 scale; remap_swing_ratio(0.34)=0.67 internal = triplet swing — the v2 value 0.67 was on the internal scale and would have produced a 0.835 hard shuffle).

m4_peak_build_1.name: m4_peak_build_1
m4_peak_build_1.notes: IV. Full Grid (build) — tempo steps to 116 in Logic tempo track at this section boundary. | Harmony fix: V5 truncated (vi-ii-v) — descending-fourths build, withholds I into the hold section. | Decouple pass (v3): melody=motif (sole owner of the cell), bass=steady (locked figure from STEADY_FIGURES, chord- and meter-bound, approach notes into changes), harmony=motif (stressed filter on the shaped v3 cell -> five varied-length on-beat comp chords per 2-bar cycle — its own syncopated life against the bass), swing=0.34 at section level (public 0.0-1.0 scale; remap_swing_ratio(0.34)=0.67 internal = triplet swing — the v2 value 0.67 was on the internal scale and would have produced a 0.835 hard shuffle).

m4_peak_hold_1.name: m4_peak_hold_1
m4_peak_hold_1.notes: Anchor — the vi-ii-v-I descending-fourth chain (theme's stated primary high-density vehicle), unaltered. Full sustained color-field pad, straight groove.

piece.notes: v7 — restructured from v6's 23-section narrative arc into song form. Uses ONLY the first 8 sections of v6 (m1_grid_1 through m4_peak_hold_1 — the opening/build arc, before dissolve/return), unchanged in every field, looped 5x via the 'form' array to reach ~28.6 min at 98 BPM (140 bars/cycle x 5 = 700 bars, no section stretched). Each pass generates fresh material under the same style/density/harmony parameters (default song-form seed offsetting, not exact_repeat), so the 5 loops vary generatively while staying in the same aesthetic register — this is now a static/circular piece (the grid as an ambient loop) rather than the v6 arc-with-dissolution. If you want the original arc shape back, v6 is still the file for that.
